Director's Report — BTC Health Practice: Dr James Mitchell

1. Background

Dr James Mitchell, residing at 42 Harbour Road, Manly NSW 2095, has operated an orthopaedic surgical practice through BTC Health Australia since July 2018. The practice specialises in allograft tissue procedures and the supply of surgical consumables to private hospitals across metropolitan Sydney. Dr Mitchell holds provider number 2834710F registered with Medicare Australia for orthopaedic surgical services.

2. Financial Overview

For the financial year ended 30 June 2023, the practice reported gross revenue of $573,230.31, a decline of approximately 18% on the prior year. Dr Mitchell attributes this decline primarily to the termination of a key supply agreement with BlueShak Pty Ltd effective 15 March 2024, which accounted for roughly $516,000 in annual revenue. The practice maintained separate banking arrangements through its operational account (BSB 062-000 Account 12345678) held with Commonwealth Bank of Australia.

3. Creditor Exposure and Distress Events

Since 1 July 2023, the practice has accumulated significant trade creditor arrears. The Mitchell Family Trust, which holds the practice premises at Manly, is owed $87,500 in unpaid rent. Prospa Advance has an outstanding loan balance of $142,000 secured against practice equipment.

The termination by BlueShak Pty Ltd of the allograft tissue supply agreement created an immediate cash flow crisis. Dr Mitchell was unable to source alternative surgical consumables at comparable pricing, leading to a reduction in theatre bookings and a corresponding decline in revenue. Prior to termination, revenue from allograft tissue procedures averaged $48,000 per month, with surgical consumables contributing a further $12,000 per month.

4. Current Position

As at the date of this report, Dr Mitchell's practice has total liabilities of approximately $412,000 against realisable assets valued at $185,000. The practice continues to trade on a limited basis, performing approximately three orthopaedic procedures per week compared to the historical average of eight.

The directors believe that a Small Business Restructuring plan offers the best prospect of a better return to creditors than would be achieved through immediate liquidation. Under the proposed plan, contributions totalling $210,000 would be made over a 36-month period from ongoing surgical practice revenue, representing a return of approximately 51 cents in the dollar to unsecured creditors.

5. Trading History and Supplier Relationships

Prior to the BTC Health Australia supply agreement termination, the practice maintained healthy margins. The BlueShak Pty Ltd agreement provided preferential pricing on titanium fixation devices and surgical mesh products. Following the termination on 15 March 2024, Dr Mitchell approached several alternative suppliers including Prospa Advance for bridging finance. However, the terms offered were significantly less favourable than those previously enjoyed under the BlueShak arrangement.

The Mitchell Family Trust has agreed to defer rent obligations for a period of twelve months to support the restructuring plan. This concession, valued at $87,500, demonstrates the commitment of related parties to achieving a successful outcome for all creditors.

6. Director's Declaration

Dr James Mitchell confirms that the information provided in this report is true and correct to the best of his knowledge. All patient records and clinical documentation relating to orthopaedic surgical services have been maintained in accordance with applicable medical standards and regulatory requirements.

Dr Mitchell has engaged LexSolv AI to assist with the preparation of this restructuring plan and the associated Declaration of Relevant Relationships and Indemnities.
